purpose: Identifying the current stock-bond correlation regime and inferring portfolio construction implications by mapping the full chronology of six correlation regimes from 1942 to present.
components: Six identified regimes from 1942 to present
Two prior split-correlation episodes: 1965-69 (resolved to fiscal/inflation dominance) and 2003-06 (resolved to monetary dominance)
Current state: 2Y-SPX negative (-0.25 to -0.35), 30Y-SPX positive (+0.05 to +0.15)
Resolution timeline: 2-4 years in both prior episodes
Portfolio implication: long-duration Treasuries losing hedging property for equity portfolios
current_assessment: Current split is a transitional state. The 1965-69 resolution pathway (toward fiscal dominance) is more probable given structural deficits, but the 2003-06 counterexample prevents high conviction. N=2 is explicitly insufficient for statistical inference.
limitations: N=2 for prior split-correlation episodes — insufficient for statistical inference
Prior episodes had different institutional contexts (pre-QE, different Fed frameworks)
Framework identifies the regime but cannot predict resolution direction
Value is in the framework itself, not in directional prediction
